heading: Electronic and information technology
label: 794d Electronic and information technology

children.heading: Requirements for Federal departments and agencies
children.label: a Requirements for Federal departments and agencies

children.heading: Development, procurement, maintenance, or use of electronic and information technology
children.label: A Development, procurement, maintenance, or use of electronic and information technology
i
children.text: individuals with disabilities who are Federal employees to have access to and use of information and data that is comparable to the access to and use of the information and data by Federal employees who are not individuals with disabilities; and

children.label: ii
children.text: individuals with disabilities who are members of the public seeking information or services from a Federal department or agency to have access to and use of information and data that is comparable to the access to and use of the information and data by such members of the public who are not individuals with disabilities.

children.heading: Alternative means efforts
children.label: B Alternative means efforts
children.text: When development, procurement, maintenance, or use of electronic and information technology that meets the standards published by the Access Board under paragraph (2) would impose an undue burden, the Federal department or agency shall provide individuals with disabilities covered by paragraph (1) with the information and data involved by an alternative means of access that allows the individual to use the information and data.

children.label: i
children.text: for purposes of this section, a definition of electronic and information technology that is consistent with the definition of information technology specified in ; andsection 11101(6) of title 40

children.label: ii
children.text: the technical and functional performance criteria necessary to implement the requirements set forth in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Review and amendment
children.label: B Review and amendment
children.text: The Access Board shall periodically review and, as appropriate, amend the standards required under subparagraph (A) to reflect technological advances or changes in electronic and information technology.

children.heading: Incorporation of standards
children.label: 3 Incorporation of standards
children.text: Not later than 6 months after the Access Board publishes the standards required under paragraph (2), the Federal Acquisition Regulatory Council shall revise the Federal Acquisition Regulation and each Federal department or agency shall revise the Federal procurement policies and directives under the control of the department or agency to incorporate those standards. Not later than 6 months after the Access Board revises any standards required under paragraph (2), the Council shall revise the Federal Acquisition Regulation and each appropriate Federal department or agency shall revise the procurement policies and directives, as necessary, to incorporate the revisions.

children.heading: Acquisition planning
children.label: 4 Acquisition planning
children.text: In the event that a Federal department or agency determines that compliance with the standards issued by the Access Board under paragraph (2) relating to procurement imposes an undue burden, the documentation by the department or agency supporting the procurement shall explain why compliance creates an undue burden.

children.heading: Exemption for national security systems
children.label: 5 Exemption for national security systems
children.text: This section shall not apply to national security systems, as that term is defined in .section 11103(a) of title 40

children.label: i
children.text: to make equipment owned by the Federal Government available for access and use by individuals with disabilities covered by paragraph (1) at a location other than that where the electronic and information technology is provided to the public; or

children.label: ii
children.text: to purchase equipment for access and use by individuals with disabilities covered by paragraph (1) at a location other than that where the electronic and information technology is provided to the public.

children.heading: Software and peripheral devices
children.label: B Software and peripheral devices
children.text: Except as required to comply with standards issued by the Access Board under paragraph (2), nothing in paragraph (1) requires the installation of specific accessibility-related software or the attachment of a specific accessibility-related peripheral device at a workstation of a Federal employee who is not an individual with a disability.

children.heading: Technical assistance
children.label: b Technical assistance
children.text: The Administrator of General Services and the Access Board shall provide technical assistance to individuals and Federal departments and agencies concerning the requirements of this section.

children.heading: Agency evaluations
children.label: c Agency evaluations
children.text: Not later than 6 months after , the head of each Federal department or agency shall evaluate the extent to which the electronic and information technology of the department or agency is accessible to and usable by individuals with disabilities described in subsection (a)(1), compared to the access to and use of the technology by individuals described in such subsection who are not individuals with disabilities, and submit a report containing the evaluation to the Attorney General.August 7, 1998

children.heading: Interim report
children.label: 1 Interim report
children.text: Not later than 18 months after , the Attorney General shall prepare and submit to the President a report containing information on and recommendations regarding the extent to which the electronic and information technology of the Federal Government is accessible to and usable by individuals with disabilities described in subsection (a)(1).August 7, 1998

children.heading: Biennial reports
children.label: 2 Biennial reports
children.text: Not later than 3 years after , and every 2 years thereafter, the Attorney General shall prepare and submit to the President and Congress a report containing information on and recommendations regarding the state of Federal department and agency compliance with the requirements of this section, including actions regarding individual complaints under subsection (f).August 7, 1998

children.heading: Cooperation
children.label: e Cooperation
children.text: Each head of a Federal department or agency (including the Access Board, the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission, and the General Services Administration) shall provide to the Attorney General such information as the Attorney General determines is necessary to conduct the evaluations under subsection (c) and prepare the reports under subsection (d).

children.heading: Complaints
children.label: A Complaints
children.text: Effective 6 months after the date of publication by the Access Board of final standards described in subsection (a)(2), any individual with a disability may file a complaint alleging that a Federal department or agency fails to comply with subsection (a)(1) in providing electronic and information technology.

children.heading: Application
children.label: B Application
children.text: This subsection shall apply only to electronic and information technology that is procured by a Federal department or agency not less than 6 months after the date of publication by the Access Board of final standards described in subsection (a)(2).

children.heading: Administrative complaints
children.label: 2 Administrative complaints
children.text: Complaints filed under paragraph (1) shall be filed with the Federal department or agency alleged to be in noncompliance. The Federal department or agency receiving the complaint shall apply the complaint procedures established to implement for resolving allegations of discrimination in a federally conducted program or activity.section 794 of this title

children.heading: Civil actions
children.label: 3 Civil actions
children.text: The remedies, procedures, and rights set forth in sections 794a(a)(2) and 794a(b) of this title shall be the remedies, procedures, and rights available to any individual with a disability filing a complaint under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Application to other Federal laws
children.label: g Application to other Federal laws
children.text: This section shall not be construed to limit any right, remedy, or procedure otherwise available under any provision of Federal law (including sections 791 through 794a of this title) that provides greater or equal protection for the rights of individuals with disabilities than this section.
